Communication Between the Uniface and TM Middleware

The interaction and communication between Uniface, transaction management (TM) middleware, and the DBMS is via specific interfaces and connectors.

The following illustration shows how a Uniface application communicates with a database via TM middleware and a TM-controlled Uniface Application Server.

The main components are:

  • Uniface client application

    The application defines the boundaries of a transaction and specifies what constitutes a transaction. Uniface TM support requires that all TM-controlled DBMS connectors be located on (TM-controlled) servers and not on client machines.

    The Uniface application can directly access DBMS connectors, but these connectors are not TM-controlled and can only use local transactions.
  • Universal Request Broker (URB) architecture

    The URB allows applications that run with Uniface native middleware to be executed on enterprise middleware without changing the application components. Enterprise middleware offers multi-database and heterogeneous server updating, message queuing, transaction load balancing and automatic transaction routing.

  • Middleware with a transaction interface

    The middleware is third-party transaction processing software that manages

    global transactions. The TM coordinates the decision to start, commit, or rollback global transactions. When a DBMS connector is TM-controlled, the TM middleware is the only software allowed to send connect, disconnect, begin transaction, commit and rollback requests to the DBMS.

    The middleware presents Uniface with a transaction interface and a communication (ICC) interface. The transaction interface is used by the Uniface transaction manager connectors to send transaction manager requests. The communication interface is used to connect components, for example Uniface clients and servers.

  • Middleware-specific Uniface Server

    This is a Uniface Server that is custom-made for a specific middleware product. The server includes an extra interface, the middleware access point, which the middleware uses to connect to the Uniface Server.

  • DBMS

    The DBMS provides an SQL interface used by DBMS connectors, which is the standard DBMS API, and a transaction interface, used by middleware to issue transaction, connect, and disconnect requests to the DBMS

The main connectors and interfaces are:

  • Uniface ICC Interface and connector

    Uniface provides middleware-independent support for connecting Uniface components via the Inter-Component Communication (ICC) interface (which is middleware-independent).

    The ICC interface and connector connect the Uniface application to a middleware-specific Uniface application server. The Uniface network interface and connectors cannot be used to connect to a middleware-specific application server.

    The ICC connector allows Uniface to communicate with middleware software, translating Uniface ProcScript statement activate calls into middleware-specific calls.

    Uniface provides you with a means to develop your own middleware connector. The ICC API is based on a C language 3GL interface.

  • TM interface and TM connector

    To provide transaction manager support, Uniface supplies the TM interface, which is middleware and protocol-independent and TM connectors, which are middleware-specific and protocol-specific.

    The TM interface supports TP protocols for communication between a transaction manager, server, and DBMS. A TP protocol is a standard that defines how to communicate during transaction processing. The middleware, TM connector, DBMS connector, and DBMS must all support the same TP protocol. Uniface supports the TX X/Open group Distributed Transaction Processing (DTP) model protocol.

  • TM access point of the Application Server

    This is the entry point through which the middleware calls services on the Application Server.

  • DBMS interface and custom DBMS connector

    Uniface provides support for TM-controlled DBMSs via a Uniface DBMS interface (the standard Uniface DBMS connector interface) and a custom TM-controlled DBMS connector. The DBMS connector is one which provides TM support, and is TM-controlled.

    TM-controlled DBMS connectors allow the middleware to control the transactions. They do not receive commit and rollback requests from the Uniface kernel, nor are they allowed to send any transaction manager, connect or disconnect requests to the DBMS.
